name: Orange Butter Thins
recipeInstructions:
My mother, of Welsh heritage, enjoyed a mid-afternoon sip of tea accompanied by light cookies. These were her favorite.
Cream butter and sugar in a large bowl until smooth. Add the slightly beaten egg and mix well. Stir in flour, salt, and grated orange rind until combined.
Cover dough with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place a small walnut-sized ball of batter onto a lightly greased cookie sheet. Cover with a square of wax paper.
Press the dough flat using the bottom of a glass, ensuring it is very thin. Bake for 5 minutes or until light brown.
Remove cookies from the baking sheet using a spatula and let cool on the wax paper. These yield approximately 4 dozen cookies.
